message.content:
Adversarially verify this finding about the repo at /home/jleechan/projects/ez-gh-actions (public: github.com/jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions, evidence at /home/jleechan/projects/ez-gh-actions/evidence/e2e-20260703, run https://github.com/jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions/actions/runs/28685531107). Try to REFUTE it by reading the actual code/evidence and, where relevant, live gh queries. Finding from "code-bugs" (critical): free_disk_gb() checks the HOST filesystem at the DAEMON's path — disk-floor guard silently fails open in exactly the VM setups the tool targets
docker_backend.rs:159-181. `free_disk_gb()` asks the daemon for `{{.DockerRootDir}}` (a path INSIDE the daemon's mount namespace, e.g. `/var/lib/docker` inside the Colima/Lima/Docker Desktop VM) and then runs host-side `df -Pk` against that string. Three concrete failures: (1) macOS Docker Desktop / Colima: `/var/lib/docker` does not exist on the host, `df` exits non-zero, function returns `None`, and `ensure_count` (docker_backend.rs:192) SKIPS the disk check entirely — the guard the code comments call 'the dominant self-hosted runner failure mode' is a silent no-op. (2) Linux host that also has a native `/var/lib/docker` directory but uses a Colima context: `df` measures the host root fs (plenty free) while the VM disk is 100% full — guard passes, spawns more runners onto a full VM disk, jobs fail with ENOSPC. This is precisely the 2026-06-14 colima disk-fill incident class. (3) daemon_capacity() at docker_backend.rs:36 already documents that the daemon may be a VM, so the codebase knows about this and still gets it wrong for disk.
Refute if the factual basis is wrong, the scenario cannot occur, or severity is inflated (severity inflation alone -> not refuted, but say so).

Output too large (4.9KB). Full output saved to: /home/jleechan/.claude/projects/-home-jleechan-projects-worktree-runner/8ca589dd-8422-44b1-a543-52bd7b71f14b/tool-results/b0k5do65n.txt

Preview (first 2KB):
use std::fs::OpenOptions;
use std::process::Command;

/// What this host can offer, detected at runtime.
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct Platform {
    pub os: &'static str,
    pub arch: &'static str,
    /// /dev/kvm exists AND this user can open it read-write.
    pub kvm_usable: bool,
    pub has_tart: bool,
    pub has_virsh: bool,
    /// Docker CLI present and the daemon answered.
    pub docker_ok: bool,
    /// sysbox-runc registered as a Docker runtime.
    pub sysbox_runtime: bool,
    pub total_mem_mb: u64,
    pub cpus: u32,
}

pub fn detect() -> Platform {
    let os = if cfg!(target_os = "macos") {
        "macos"
    } else if cfg!(target_os = "linux") {
        "linux"
    } else {
        "unsupported"
    };

    Platform {
        os,
        arch: std::env::consts::ARCH,
        kvm_usable: kvm_usable(),
        has_tart: which::which("tart").is_ok(),
        has_virsh: which::which("virsh").is_ok(),
        docker_ok: docker_daemon_ok(),
        sysbox_runtime: sysbox_runtime_present(),
        total_mem_mb: total_mem_mb(),
        cpus: std::thread::available_parallelism()
            .map(|n| n.get() as u32)
            .unwrap_or(1),
    }
}

/// Existence alone is not enough: the user must be in the kvm group (or have
/// an ACL) for the device to be usable, so try to actually open it.
fn kvm_usable() -> bool {
    OpenOptions::new()
        .read(true)
        .write(true)
        .open("/dev/kvm")
        .is_ok()
}

fn docker_daemon_ok() -> bool {
    Command::new("docker")
        .args(["version", "--format", "{{.Server.Version}}"])
        .output()
        .map(|o| o.status.success())
        .unwrap_or(false)
}

fn sysbox_runtime_present() -> bool {
    Command::new("docker")
        .args(["info", "--format", "{{json .Runtimes}}"])
        .output()
        .map(|o| o.status.success() && String::from_utf8_lossy(&o.stdout).contains("sysbox-runc"))
        .unwrap_or(false)
}

fn total_mem_mb() -> u64 {
...
</persisted-output>

# ez-gh-actions E2E Evidence — real ephemeral runner, real GitHub Actions job

- Repo: https://github.com/jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions
- Commit under test: 5ef5406779925598565c8d277d13b865db78947a (fix c27d389 applied mid-run after E2E caught the daemon-capacity bug)
- Actions run (REAL, on our runner): https://github.com/jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions/actions/runs/28685531107
- Host: Jeff-Ubuntu (linux x86_64, 32 cpus/64GB), docker daemon = 4-cpu/12GB Lima VM

## Proof chain
1. `01_init.txt` — ezgha init: daemon-aware limits (5977 MB / 2 cpus / 512 pids)
2. `02_doctor.txt` — capability detection (docker ok, kvm ok, virsh/tart/sysbox missing, gh auth ok)
3. `03_start.txt` — started ephemeral runner ezgha-Jeff-Ubuntu-6a4833a11c652b
4. `04_status_before_job.txt` — container 1143cf62e74f running; GitHub runner #3 status=online
5. `06_run_watch.txt` — workflow_dispatch run 28685531107 completed success
6. `07_job_log.txt` — job output: runner name = ezgha-Jeff-Ubuntu-6a4833a11c652b; job hostname = 1143cf62e74f (== our container ID); memory.max = 6267338752 (= 5977 MB exactly); pids.max = 512
7. `08_status_after_job.txt` — 0 containers, 0 registered runners (ephemeral: JIT deregistered + --rm cleanup)

## Bug found and fixed by this E2E
`docker run --cpus 16` failed: daemon (Lima VM) has 4 cpus while host has 32.
Fix c27d389: limits derived from `docker info` NCPU/MemTotal + clamped at start.
Failure-path cleanup verified: the orphaned JIT registration was auto-removed.

input.reasoning:
NOT REFUTED — confirmed with code reading plus a live reproduction on the exact host class the tool's own evidence bundle used.

Factual basis verified line-by-line:
1. /home/jleechan/projects/ez-gh-actions/src/docker_backend.rs:159-181 — `free_disk_gb()` gets `{{.DockerRootDir}}` from `docker info` (a daemon-namespace path) and runs host-side `df -Pk` against that string; on df failure it returns `None`.
2. docker_backend.rs:192-200 — `ensure_count` uses `if let Some(free) = free_disk_gb()`, so `None` silently skips the disk floor entirely (fail-open), while the function's own comment (lines 184-186) calls disk exhaustion "the dominant self-hosted runner failure mode."
3. docker_backend.rs:33-35 — `daemon_capacity()`'s doc comment explicitly acknowledges the daemon may live in a Colima/Lima/Docker Desktop VM; CPU/memory are daemon-derived but disk is not, exactly as the finding says.

Live reproduction of failure mode (2), on this machine (same host class as the evidence bundle's "Jeff-Ubuntu ... docker daemon = 4-cpu/12GB Lima VM"): active docker context is `lima-colima`; `docker info` reports DockerRootDir=/var/lib/docker (Ubuntu 24.04 VM daemon); the host ALSO has a native root-owned /var/lib/docker; host `df -Pk /var/lib/docker` succeeds and reports the HOST root fs: ~497 GB free (41% used). Inside the VM, `limactl shell colima -- df -Pk /var/lib/docker` shows /dev/vda1 with only ~28 GB free (76% used). So free_disk_gb() currently overreports free space by ~470 GB on the very machine the e2e evidence (run 28685531107) validated — if the VM disk filled, the guard would still pass and spawn runners. This matches the 2026-06-14 colima disk-fill incident class.

Failure mode (1) (macOS Docker Desktop/Colima) also holds by inspection: /var/lib/docker does not exist on a macOS host, `df` exits non-zero, `free_disk_gb()` returns None, and ensure_count skips the check silently. macOS is an explicitly supported platform (src/platform.rs detects target_os = "macos"; README/DESIGN list Tart/macOS on the roadmap), and Docker on macOS always runs in a VM, so the guard is a guaranteed no-op there.

Severity is not inflated: the guard's sole purpose (per the code's own comment) is the dominant failure mode, and it is either a silent no-op (macOS) or measures the wrong filesystem (Linux+Colima — the tool's own validated deployment environment). Critical/high is justified.
